summary: Required base-page composition for standard authenticated applications, with one ShellApp coordinating primary destinations, secondary route sections, tools chrome, responsive modes, and stable main content geometry.
useWhen: Building any standard authenticated product or workspace page with CompoMo.
Application navigation and tools must remain consistent around changing route content.
Creating a reusable base-page template for CompoMo applications.
avoidWhen: Building authentication, onboarding, public, marketing, print, error, or intentionally focused full-screen experiences.
The page is already rendered inside an ShellApp.
A nested or second application shell is being considered.

components.role: Provides the single workspace root and coordinates shell composition, shared chrome, responsive modes, and stable content geometry.
Provides the application's one primary navigation with top-level destinations and standard Dashboard/Settings and account footer controls.
Provides secondary route-section navigation or the active primary destination label while preserving shell height.
Keeps each product tool owner mounted once while adapting desktop/tablet rail and drawer chrome to full-stage mobile Search, Agents, and Inbox surfaces.
Browses Dashboard and Settings primary areas in the mobile stage without navigating until an area is selected.
Renders the active area's secondary routes in a horizontally scrollable mobile row.
Provides persistent Menu, current area, Search, Agents, and Inbox destinations below 768px.

stateOwnership: The application router owns the current route, Dashboard or Settings context, authorization filtering, destination and section collections, localized labels, and navigation execution.

ShellApp owns desktop, tablet, and mobile composition, shell geometry, navStyle and responsive-mode propagation, gradient alignment, chrome transitions, metrics, inert stage boundaries, and shell shortcuts.
PanelNav owns primary-destination presentation and the user-controlled desktop collapse preference.
BarNav derives the active secondary route from currentUrl and basePath and receives the active PanelNav destination label as heading.
The application owns mobileDestination, mobileNavigationOpen, the actual active tool, and the last Inbox segment; ShellTools preserves product owners and presentation.
The application router owns only the routed workspace. Search, Agents, Inbox, and Menu changes never change router history.
PanelNav groups and BarNav dividers visually organize peers and never introduce nested navigation hierarchy.
accessibility: Render one main landmark in the default content region.
Use PanelNav exclusively for primary destinations and BarNav exclusively for secondary route sections.
Keep localized visible labels and accessible names available when navigation is collapsed or moved into overflow.
Provide visible labels for all five mobile-bar items and Search and Help in the available shell actions.
Preserve keyboard focus and route state across responsive and Dashboard/Settings context changes.
responsiveBehavior: Use fixed system breakpoints: desktop at 1200px and wider, tablet from 768px through 1199px, and mobile below 768px.
Desktop permits PanelNav expansion and collapse and restores the persisted user preference.
Tablet keeps PanelNav permanently collapsed and does not overwrite the desktop preference.
Mobile hides and inerts PanelNav and BarNav, removes the gradient wash, and uses a solid primary stage above a safe-area-aware persistent bottom bar.
The routed workspace stays mounted and is only concealed and inert while a global surface or navigation pane occupies the stage.
Messages, Stacks, and Activity map to mobile Inbox and preserve the selected segment across destination and breakpoint changes.

recipes.title: Responsive application shell
recipes.summary: One routed workspace and one set of persistent tool owners shared across desktop, tablet, and mobile presentations.

files.content:
<ds-shell-app id="shell" mobile-destination="area">
  <ds-panel-nav id="panel" slot="panel"></ds-panel-nav>
  <ds-shell-mobile-nav id="mobile-nav" slot="mobile-navigation"></ds-shell-mobile-nav>
  <ds-bar-nav id="bar" slot="bar"></ds-bar-nav>
  <ds-shell-mobile-section-nav id="mobile-sections" slot="mobile-section-nav"></ds-shell-mobile-section-nav>
  <ds-shell-tools id="tools" slot="tools">
    <app-search slot="search-view"></app-search>
    <app-agents slot="agents-view"></app-agents>
    <app-messages slot="messages-view"></app-messages>
    <app-stacks slot="stacks-view"></app-stacks>
    <app-activity slot="activity-view"></app-activity>
    <app-help slot="help-view"></app-help>
  </ds-shell-tools>
  <main><!-- routed workspace --></main>
  <ds-shell-mobile-bar id="mobile-bar" slot="mobile-bar"></ds-shell-mobile-bar>
</ds-shell-app>

import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-shell-app.js'
import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-panel-nav.js'
import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-bar-nav.js'
import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-shell-tools.js'
import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-shell-mobile-nav.js'
import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-shell-mobile-section-nav.js'
import '@ds-mo/ui/components/ds-shell-mobile-bar.js'
import type { ShellMobileDestination } from '@ds-mo/ui/shell'

const shell = document.querySelector<HTMLDsShellAppElement>('#shell')!
const bar = document.querySelector<HTMLDsShellMobileBarElement>('#mobile-bar')!
const mobileNav = document.querySelector<HTMLDsShellMobileNavElement>('#mobile-nav')!

function setMobileState(destination: ShellMobileDestination, navigationOpen = false) {
  shell.mobileDestination = destination
  shell.mobileNavigationOpen = navigationOpen
  bar.activeDestination = destination
  bar.navigationExpanded = navigationOpen
  mobileNav.open = navigationOpen
}

bar.addEventListener('dsNavigationToggle', event => setMobileState(shell.mobileDestination, event.detail))
bar.addEventListener('dsDestinationChange', event => setMobileState(event.detail.destination))

notes: Assign navigation groups, tabs, items, headers, and current-area data as JavaScript properties.
The router outlet remains in the default slot and global destination changes do not navigate.

recipes.title: Responsive application shell
recipes.summary: A controlled React shell coordinator with stable routed and tool children.
notes: Keep routed and tool-owner element identity stable; control only shell visibility and selection state.

recipes.title: Responsive application shell
recipes.summary: A controlled Angular shell using generated standalone adapters and native slot names.

files.content:
<ds-shell-app [mobileDestination]="destination" [mobileNavigationOpen]="navigationOpen">
  <ds-panel-nav slot="panel"></ds-panel-nav>
  <ds-shell-mobile-nav slot="mobile-navigation" [open]="navigationOpen"></ds-shell-mobile-nav>
  <ds-bar-nav slot="bar"></ds-bar-nav>
  <ds-shell-mobile-section-nav slot="mobile-section-nav"></ds-shell-mobile-section-nav>
  <ds-shell-tools slot="tools"><app-search slot="search-view" /></ds-shell-tools>
  <router-outlet />
  <ds-shell-mobile-bar slot="mobile-bar" [activeDestination]="destination" [navigationExpanded]="navigationOpen" (dsNavigationToggle)="navigationOpen = $event.detail" (dsDestinationChange)="selectDestination($event.detail.destination)"></ds-shell-mobile-bar>
</ds-shell-app>

import { Component } from '@angular/core'
import { RouterOutlet } from '@angular/router'
import type { ShellMobileDestination } from '@ds-mo/ui/shell'
import { DsBarNav } from '@ds-mo/ui/angular/ds-bar-nav'
import { DsPanelNav } from '@ds-mo/ui/angular/ds-panel-nav'
import { DsShellApp } from '@ds-mo/ui/angular/ds-shell-app'
import { DsShellMobileBar } from '@ds-mo/ui/angular/ds-shell-mobile-bar'
import { DsShellMobileNav } from '@ds-mo/ui/angular/ds-shell-mobile-nav'
import { DsShellMobileSectionNav } from '@ds-mo/ui/angular/ds-shell-mobile-section-nav'
import { DsShellTools } from '@ds-mo/ui/angular/ds-shell-tools'

@Component({ selector: 'app-shell', standalone: true, imports: [RouterOutlet, DsBarNav, DsPanelNav, DsShellApp, DsShellMobileBar, DsShellMobileNav, DsShellMobileSectionNav, DsShellTools], templateUrl: './app-shell.component.html' })
export class AppShellComponent {
  destination: ShellMobileDestination = 'area'
  navigationOpen = false
  selectDestination(destination: ShellMobileDestination) { this.navigationOpen = false; this.destination = destination }
}

notes: Bind structured navigation and tool data as properties and update router-derived inputs together.
